caption:
"The UIC Black Cultural Center knows that the start of new school year can be a hectic time for everyone, so here are a few things we think will help with the transition from summer to school:

1) Alarms: 
- set them to wake up, have a snack break, time your studying, take a nap, etc.— alarms are a helpful way to make sure you complete things in a timely manner 

2) Notebooks & Writing Utensils: 
- every class might not require one and you might not be a pen-to-paper kind of note-taker, but having a physical notebook and some pens to write with can be super helpful!

3) Laptop and/or Tablet
- most, if not all, classes require a reliable device to access class platforms like Blackboard, Perusall, Moodle, etc.so make sure you find/have a laptop, tablet, or dependable device! 
(visit satech.uic.edu, it.uic.edu, or the UIC library to learn more about alternative access to technology equipment)

4) SNACKS!
- it’s easy to get lost in the hustle and bustle of the day and even week, but you HAVE to eat AND stay hydrated— so if you’re on the go, grab a fruit or one of those adult “lunchables”, make a sandwich, keep some snack bars, a water bottle (reusable is highly recommended 🌎❗️), and make sure you eat!
(visit wellnesscenter.uic.edu to learn more about resources and services regarding food support)

5) Class GroupMe and group chats:
- we get it; talking to people– new or not– can be awkward and intimidating, but joining class chats (no matter the platform) can help you stay informed about what’s going on in class and connect with peers that might be experiencing the same things as you

6) Agenda Tracker
- the class schedule, events happening around campus, birthdays, assignments, and more…it’s all gotta go somewhere! From gCal to a physical planner or reminder sticky notes around your room– find what works for you to keep track of what you have going on in your life

If you ever need a place to help you connect with or locate resources, or even somewhere to destress and hang out, we invite you to come see us at THE Black Cultural Center! 🎉".

caption:
"🪑 Special Edition Black Table Talk 
UIC BCC x DCC: Reading Discussion of Sami Schalk’s “𝑩𝒍𝒂𝒄𝒌 𝑫𝒊𝒔𝒂𝒃𝒊𝒍𝒊𝒕𝒚 𝑷𝒐𝒍𝒊𝒕𝒊𝒄𝒔”

Join us Wednesday, April 17th, 2024 from 12pm to 1pm in the BCC Gallery and on Zoom 
( https://go.uic.edu/DCCZoom )
to discuss excerpts from Dr. Schalk’s
𝑩𝒍𝒂𝒄𝒌 𝑫𝒊𝒔𝒂𝒃𝒊𝒍𝒊𝒕𝒚 𝑷𝒐𝒍𝒊𝒕𝒊𝒄𝒔, the connections it draws, and the questions it provokes. 

⭐️ Book Giveaway of Dr. Schalk’s, 𝑩𝒍𝒂𝒄𝒌 𝑫𝒊𝒔𝒂𝒃𝒊𝒍𝒊𝒕𝒚 𝑷𝒐𝒍𝒊𝒕𝒊𝒄𝒔 while supplies generously sponsored by ACCESS LIVING Arts & Culture Project last!

⭐️ Lunch will be provided! 
(we will have boxed/take-away friendly vegan and gluten-free options)

This event is hosted by UIC’s Black Cultural Center, Disability Cultural Center, and the Provost Initiative on the Racialized Body. 
Co-sponsors:
- ACCESS LIVING Arts & Culture Project 
- UIC Department of Black Studies

🚨⚠️COVID SAFETY⚠️🚨
More masking means more safety for immunocompromised people in our communities. Because lunch will be served, people will likely be unmasked to eat and drink. Please wear a mask when you’re not actively eating or drinking! We will have extras on hand.

ACCESS INFORMATION: 
We will have CART (live captions) in the Zoom and projected into the space. 
The BCC gallery is on the 2nd floor, and the elevator is located in the adjacent, connected building, Burnham Hall.

Flyer 1 ID: On a fuscia background a flier features the text above and an image in the bottom right of the book, “Black Disability Politics.”
Flier 2 ID: On a fuscia background a flier features the text above and the logos of the co-sponsors mentioned above.".

caption:
"🌱🌱🌱UIC LCC x BCC x EARTH MONTH 🌱🌱🌱

In collaboration with the @bccatuic and @sustainableuic Join us for a Hazel Johnson Collage Workshop and Podcast Party on April 18th!

This event is our first ARTivism in honor of Earth Month! Join us April 18th from 1pm-3pm at the UIC Latino Cultural Center while we honor the legacy of the mother of environmental justice, Hazel Johnson.

We will be listening to Respiar Media’s podcast, Help This Garden Grow, which tells the story of Hazel Johnson, a resident of the far south side neighborhood Altgeld Gardens and founder of @pcrchi. We will create collages for environmental justice as we listen to the first two episodes of the podcast and learn from a multi-generational conversation between organizers, policymakers and land stewards. 

We will be joined by community organizers from People for Community Recovery and the Southeast Environmental Task Force! 🤝

Keep an eye out for our other Earth Month programs and we hope to see you there!

All events are free and open to the public! The LCC is wheelchair accessible! 

🌸🌸🌸
*Help This Garden Grow image from Respiar Media’s podcast Cover*".

caption:
"🧘🏾 Are you looking for a quick break amidst a busy day? Packed schedule and midterm season got you feeling all over the place? Was rest and relaxation on your New Year’s Resolution list for 2024? 

Come to the Black Cultural Center this Wednesday, March 6th from 12pm-1pm for R&B: Rest and Balance! 💆🏾✨

While the beautiful sounds of R&B play in the background, we will be reflecting and visualizing how you take care of yourself this year, from allocating time for new hobbies to squashing bad habits. Enjoy snacks, community, and tea while making Pinterest boards, creating routines, putting together a new playlist, and much more! 🪴

Bring a friend and join us as we reclaim and love our minds, bodies, and selves 😌💚

*this event is open to ALL".
